lab_requests.justification: KDIGO 2012 Section 2.1.1 defines AKI Stage 3 as creatinine exceeding 3x baseline or rising above 4.0 mg/dL. Establishing this patient's creatinine trajectory is essential to confirm AKI staging and determine whether the RRT threshold has been crossed.
Rising BUN reflects azotemia. KDIGO 2012 Section 5.3 identifies uremic complications as an absolute indication for RRT. BUN trajectory alongside creatinine determines uremic burden.
Refractory hyperkalemia is an absolute RRT indication per KDIGO 2012 Section 5.3. In oliguric AKI with tubular necrosis, potassium cannot be excreted distally.
Refractory metabolic acidosis is a KDIGO 2012 Section 5.3 absolute indication for RRT. Low bicarbonate in the setting of ATN and hemodynamic compromise determines whether CRRT is needed.
Elevated lactate confirms hemodynamic instability, the primary reason to select CRRT over intermittent hemodialysis in this critically ill patient on mechanical ventilation.

trajectory.reasoning_summary: This 46-year-old female with AKI with tubular necrosis, acute systolic CHF, and NSTEMI has creatinine 2.2 mg/dL (2x the female ULN of 1.1), confirming AKI Stage 2. BUN of 45 indicates azotemia. Potassium 5.8 mEq/L represents hyperkalemia meeting KDIGO 2012 Section 5.3 RRT threshold. Bicarbonate 16 mEq/L represents refractory metabolic acidosis, another absolute KDIGO 5.3 indication. Lactate 3.4 mmol/L confirms hemodynamic instability from CHF and NSTEMI, mandating CRRT over intermittent hemodialysis. The patient is on mechanical ventilation, making continuous modality necessary.
